zh.lifeSummary: 當Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou於1018年10月18日出生在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易麥-吉洛易安格斯時，她的父親Foulques III 'Nerra' comte d'Anjou51歲，母親Hildegarde de Haute-Lorraine de Sundgau34歲。她於1036年與Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1076年3月18日在法國布格o-達福勒-素爾-享年57歲，埋葬在法國布格-福朗-可特艾o-達。

nameMeanings.name: d'Anjou
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone from Anjou, a former province of western France (see Langevin ).

variants.name: Langevin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Henri, Jean-Louis, Pierre, Adelard, Emile, Gaston, Leodore, Lucien, Marcel.

French: habitational name, with fused definite article l', for an Angevin, i.e. for someone from Anjou, a former province of western France that was ruled by a count as an independent territory from the 10th century. The province became a property of the English Crown for fifty years at the end of the 12th century. Compare Longway and Lonsway .

History: Mathurin Langevin dit Lacroix from Le Lude in Sarthe, France, married Marie-Thérèse Martin in Quebec City, QC, in 1674. Michel Langevin from Paris, France, married Marguerite Guertin in Contrecoeur, QC, in 1714. Jean Bergevin dit Langevin from Angers in Maine-et-Loire, France, married Marie Piton in Quebec City, QC, in 1668. Charles Citoleux/Sitoleux dit Langevin from Chambellay in Maine-et-Loire, France, married Marie-Louise Harel in Montreal, QC, in 1739.

nameMeanings.name: Ermengarde
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: dite
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Dyte
meanings: English: nickname, perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English * dight, a side-form of Middle English dought ‘brave, valiant’, and possibly derived therefore from a shortened form of Old English dyhtig, dohtig ‘doughty’. Compare Doughty .

nameMeanings.name: Blanche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a nickname for a blonde, from blanche, feminine of Old French blanc ‘white’ (of Germanic origin). It came to be associated with the notion of whiteness as indicating purity, and was introduced into England as a given name by the Normans. A pale complexion combined with light hair was long an ideal of beauty in Europe (compare Modern English fair, which at first meant ‘beautiful’ and then, from the 16th century, ‘light in colouring’).

en.lifeSummary: When Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou was born on 18 October 1018, in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France, her father, Foulques III 'Nerra' comte d'Anjou, was 51 and her mother, Hildegarde de Haute-Lorraine de Sundgau, was 34. She married Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais in 1036. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 18 March 1076, in Fleurey-sur-Ouche, Côte-d'Or, Burgundy, France, at the age of 57, and was buried in Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou est née le 18 octobre 1018, à Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, France, son père, Foulques III 'Nerra' comte d'Anjou, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Hildegarde de Haute-Lorraine de Sundgau, avait 34 ans. Elle a épousé Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais en 1036. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 18 mars 1076, à Fleurey-sur-Ouche, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, France, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterrée à Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France.

Oktober 1018 in Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Foulques III 'Nerra' comte d'Anjou 51 und ihre Mutter Hildegarde de Haute-Lorraine de Sundgau 34. Sie hat 1036 Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 18. März 1076 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Fleurey-sur-Ouche, Côte-d'Or, Burgund, Frankreich und wurde in Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich begraben.

ko.Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 앤그르스
ko.lifeSummary: Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou(이)가 1018년 10월 18일 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 맹-트-로이르 앤그르스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Foulques III 'Nerra' comte d'Anjou(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hildegarde de Haute-Lorraine de Sundgau(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1036년 Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1076년 3월 18일 프랑스 부르고그니 co테-드'어 플레우리-수-우체에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e co테-드'어에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou nasceu em 18 de outubro de 1018, em Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire, França, seu pai, Foulques III 'Nerra' comte d'Anjou, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Hildegarde de Haute-Lorraine de Sundgau, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais em 1036. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 18 de março de 1076, em Fleurey-sur-Ouche, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, França, com 57 anos, e foi sepultada em Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França.

ru.Angers, Maine-et-Loire, Pays de la Loire: ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ermengarde dite Blanche d'Anjou родилась 18 октября 1018 г. в местности ангирс, меине-ет-лоир, пеис де ла лоир, Франция, её отцу по имени Foulques III 'Nerra' comte d'Anjou было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Hildegarde de Haute-Lorraine de Sundgau, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Geoffroy II 'Ferréol' de Gâstinais в 1036 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 18 марта 1076 г. в местности флеури-сур-оуч, кoте-д'ор, бургогн, Франция в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности кoте-д'ор, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция.

Murió el 18 de marzo de 1076, en Fleurey-sur-Ouche, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, Francia, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultada en Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia.
